# masonletter

The complete AI context repository for **Mason** — a twice-weekly newsletter for operators building AI systems that actually run in production.

Published Monday and Thursday. Written for operators building with AI — from solo founders to heads of operations at 500-person companies — who want to build with AI, not feel behind because of it.

Mason is published by **Quarry**. Quarry builds the AI systems Mason writes about.

**This repo is the AI's brain.** Every Mason issue is generated by loading these files as context into Claude. The documents here are authoritative.

---

## Repository Contents

```
masonletter/
├── README.md                                    ← You are here
├── mason-brand-bible-v4.md                      ← Load first. Every word written under the Mason name.
├── mason-day-type-definitions-v2.md             ← What qualifies as Monday's Build or Thursday's Move.
├── mason-voice-and-emotion.md                   ← HOW Mason sounds. Opening and closing patterns.
├── mason-finalization-agent-prompt-v6.md        ← Master prompt for the writing AI. Current.
└── mason-newsletter-template-v6.html            ← HTML email template with visual vocabulary.
```

---

## The Two Brand Promises

Every Mason issue keeps two promises:

1. **Calm.** The reader leaves less anxious than they arrived.
2. **Substance.** The reader leaves with one real concrete thing they didn't know before.

If an issue fails either promise, it doesn't ship. Skip the day instead.

---

## The One Sentence

Every issue is a stress test of:

> *"You're not behind. You just haven't been shown how to build."*

---

## The Publication Schedule

Mason publishes twice per week:

- **Monday — THE BUILD** (1,500 to 2,200 words)
  A real build, broken down. Case studies, public engineering writeups, or Quarry portfolio engagements. Authority-demonstrating.

- **Thursday — THE MOVE** (1,200 to 1,800 words)
  A specific tactical thing the reader can apply this week. Prompt patterns, workflows, decision frameworks, small systems.

Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday — nothing. Deliberate.

---

## The Visual Vocabulary (v6)

Every Mason issue follows the v6 template structure. Each issue includes:

**Always (every issue):**
- **The Benchmark** — a hero number anchoring the story at the top
- **Numbered chapter markers** — `01 / CHAPTER 01 OF 03` clay monospace labels open each chapter
- **A source block quote** — direct from the article, full-bleed clay background
- **Fig. 01 (Flow Block)** — Input → Process → Output diagram, every issue, no exceptions
- **A pull quote** — one sentence from Mason's writing that earns pullout
- **The Reply Hook** — one specific sentence inviting a reply tied to today's content
- **The Closer** — three-part bridge on black (thesis, question, handoff) — see below
- **Work With Quarry** — full CTA section with primary button and secondary link

**BUILD days also include:**
- **The Receipts** — outcome numbers on a black full-bleed block
- **The Playbook** — four-part kit (see below)

**Optional (editorial judgment, per issue):**
- **Margin note** — marginalia callout, max one per issue, for asides that would clutter the prose
- **Fig. 02** — big-number contrast (before/after), for stories with a stark two-value contrast

Mason uses a 5-type diagram system:
1. **System Flow** — architectures (data sources → AI layer → output) — default Fig. 01 shape
2. **Time Collapse** — before/after efficiency gains
3. **The Stack** — tool/model layers used in the build
4. **Cost Curve** — cost/time/headcount drops
5. **Big Number Comparison** — stark two-value contrasts — default Fig. 02 shape

---

## The Playbook (v6 — kit format, BUILD days)

The Playbook is no longer prose with subheadings. It is a four-part kit, each part rendered as a distinct visual module:

- **Part A — What you need.** Scannable checklist card with clay checkmarks. 4-6 items. Tools, time, prerequisites.
- **Part B — The build.** Numbered move cards. 4-6 cards, each with a big clay number, a 2-5 word title, and a 20-45 word description. No tutorial voice.
- **Part C — The prompt/config/command.** Code block with a clay tag label. Real, copyable content. 100-400 words.
- **Part D — What breaks.** Warning strips with clay left borders. 3-4 specific failure modes. Known-issues doc, not paragraphs of caveats.

The Playbook sits inside its own deeper-cream container (`#FAF8F3`) to signal the visual transition from story into kit.

---

## The Closer & Work With Quarry (v6)

Every issue ends with a **three-part Closer** on black, followed by a **Work With Quarry** CTA on cream.

**The Closer** (black full-bleed):
1. **Thesis line** — the one pattern-level sentence the reader should remember. Not a restatement of the hero number. Fraunces white.
2. **Question line** — an open-ended question naming the reader's current situation. Not rhetorical. Fraunces italic clay.
3. **Handoff line** — short mono bridge to the CTA below. Default: *"If it's an AI system — that's what Quarry builds."*

**Work With Quarry** (deeper cream, sits directly below the Closer):
- Positioning: *"Quarry builds the AI systems Mason writes about."*
- Sub-line: *"Solo founder, one hire, fifty people, five hundred — the work is the same. You know what you need to build. We build it with you."*
- Primary CTA: clay solid button, *"Tell us what you're building →"*, links to `{{QUARRY_INTAKE_URL}}`
- Secondary CTA: text link, *"Or see what Quarry builds →"*, links to `{{QUARRY_WORK_URL}}`

This replaces the v5 one-line soft CTA. The Closer hands off to the CTA instead of ending with a whisper. The CTA earns real visual weight because Mason's economic model is routing engaged readers to Quarry. Hiding the CTA would be dishonest to both the reader and the business.

What stays from the old rule: never aggressive, never in the middle of the issue, only at the end. One commercial moment per issue. Clear, not pushy.

---

## The Mason Enemy

Mason has one enemy: the **Anxiety Industrial Complex.**

The AI media profiting from making operators feel behind. The tool-list content farms. The LinkedIn theatre. The consulting classes that gatekeep operational AI.

Mason never names this enemy in the newsletter. Mason is the antidote by being what they are not: generous where they gatekeep, specific where they wave hands, calm where they panic, non-selling where they pitch.

---

## Visual Identity

- Primary type: **Fraunces** (display, headlines, numbers, pull quotes)
- Secondary type: **Inter** (body, subheadings)
- Code/monospace: **JetBrains Mono** (labels, metadata, section markers)
- Color palette:
  - Black (#0A0A0A) — primary text, full-bleed blocks, Closer
  - Warm cream (#F5F1E8) — main content background
  - Deeper cream (#FAF8F3) — Playbook container, diagram backgrounds, marginalia, Work With Quarry section
  - Clay terracotta (#B85C3D) — single accent, used precisely
- No AI-generated hero images. Ever.
- Real source images allowed (always credited).

Design signal: **built, not decorated.** Editorial magazine, not Substack template.

---

## Vocabulary Rules

**Banned forever:** revolutionary, game-changer, disrupt, transform, unlock, unleash, harness, supercharge, leverage (verb), synergy, ecosystem, journey, mission-critical, best-in-class, cutting-edge, seamless, magical, insane, incredible, amazing, AGI, "this changes everything," "you won't believe."

**Also banned (LinkedIn-cadence epigrams — added in v6.2):** "This isn't X. This is Y." / "X is the new Y." / "The [adj] part isn't X. The [adj] part is Y."

**Use instead:** build, ship, ran, running, system, agent, workflow, operator, judgment, evidence, receipts, in production, the recipe, hours back, replaced, retired, calm, on your side, noise, signal.

---

## Signoff

Every issue signs itself as **"— Mason"**. No personal byline. The publication is the byline.

---

## How the System Runs

Mason uses two Make.com scenarios (plus one planned):

### 1. Mason Story Selector
- Watches the Mason editorial Telegram group
- When Cherwin pastes a URL, triggers Newsletter Writer
- Lightweight

### 2. Mason Newsletter Writer
- Triggered by Story Selector webhook
- Loads all context files from this repo via GitHub raw URLs
- Fetches article content via ScraperAPI
- Generates issue with Claude Sonnet 4.5
- Sends draft to Cherwin's email for review

**GitHub raw URLs the Writer fetches:**
- `mason-brand-bible-v4.md`
- `mason-day-type-definitions-v2.md`
- `mason-voice-and-emotion.md`
- `README.md`
- `mason-finalization-agent-prompt-v6.md`
- `mason-newsletter-template-v6.html`

### 3. Mason Story Discovery (planned)
- Scheduled Sunday 9pm SGT (for Monday) and Wednesday 9pm SGT (for Thursday)
- Fetches RSS feeds, scores candidates for day-type
- Posts ranked digest to Mason Telegram group
- Currently running every 15 min — needs schedule fix

### Review & Publishing
- Drafts arrive in Cherwin's inbox
- Cherwin reviews, edits if needed
- Sends via Beehiiv when subscriber infrastructure is live

---

## Model & Prompt Choices

- **Writer model:** Claude Sonnet 4.5 (`claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`)
- **Max tokens:** 32,000 (needed for 1,500-2,200 word issues with inline HTML and diagrams)
- **Output format:** Direct HTML (the v6.3 prompt generates filled HTML directly from the v6 template, including diagrams and Playbook kit inline)

---

## The One Rule

**Every Mason issue does two things in order: filter the noise, hand over one real thing.**

Every decision in the brand bible enforces that rule. When drafts feel wrong, check against this first.

---

## This Repository Is Alive

Update when the brand sharpens. Add banned words when they surface. Add opening or closing patterns when they work unexpectedly well.

The foundation stays: the gap between watching AI happen and building AI that works is smaller than it looks. The reader is not behind, only un-shown. Mason closes that gap — one real thing at a time, twice a week.

Everything else is tuning.

---

## Version History

- **v6 — April 19, 2026** — Template reworked with numbered chapter markers, marginalia pattern, Playbook-as-kit (checklist + numbered move cards + code block + warning strips), second diagram slot (Fig. 02 big-number contrast), three-part Closer (thesis / question / handoff), and Work With Quarry CTA section with primary clay button + secondary link. Finalization prompt v6.3 with anti-hallucination rule, consultant-cadence ban, and Closer three-part documentation. Brand bible v4 removes audience size filter.
- **v5 — April 18, 2026** — Visual vocabulary locked. Hero numbers, 5 diagram types, full-bleed color blocks, Fraunces display type. Finalization prompt v5.
- **v3 — April 18, 2026** — Twice-weekly cadence locked. Quarry positioning added. Day-types simplified to BUILD + MOVE only. Finalization prompt v4.
- **v2 — April 17, 2026** — Daily cadence attempt. JSON output experiment (abandoned).
- **v1 — April 17, 2026** — Initial drafts, strategy conversations.
